items.title: 'This was something new': What people in Greenland told us about learning to live with wildfires
items.summary: After several quieter years, wildfires have returned to western Greenland.

items.title: How proteins are inserted into cell membranes
items.summary: Researchers from Heinrich Heine University Düsseldorf (HHU) have—in collaboration with colleagues from Ludwig Maximilian University (LMU) in Munich—analyzed the complex biochemical processes that bacteria use to insert proteins into their cell membranes. They explain that—contrary to prior assumptio

items.title: Planned 1.7 million satellites 'devastating' for astronomy: Study
items.summary: The 1.7 million satellites that companies are aiming to launch into Earth's orbit in the coming years will have "devastating consequences for astronomy," new research warned Wednesday.

items.title: When companies face hostile takeover threats, they turn to ESG, and the whole community benefits
items.summary: When a company faces the prospect of a hostile takeover, its board may reach for traditional anti-takeover defenses. "Poison pills," for instance, allow existing shareholders to buy additional shares at a discount, diluting a would-be acquirer's stake and making the target more expensive to abs

items.title: Optical writing of antiferromagnets points toward new storage devices and energy efficient information systems
items.summary: A German-Japanese research team involving the University of Augsburg has made a significant breakthrough in the use of antiferromagnets. For the first time, the team has succeeded in writing magnetic information using only ultrashort laser pulses—without the need for electric currents or magnetic fi

items.title: Europe's baked rice bowl seeks escape from drought
items.summary: Summer came too early to Europe's most important rice-growing region, and weeds are taking over Sharon Angoli's parched paddies in Italy's Pavia province.

items.title: Egipat objavio 2 velika otkrića. Pronađen grad star 1600 godina i 18 grobnica
items.summary: EGIPAT objavio dva velika arheološka otkrića: očuvano bizantsko naselje u oazi Dakhla i 18 drevnih grobnica kod Aleksandrije. Otkrića pružaju uvid u svakodnevni život i jačaju turizam.

items.title: Ancient teeth from Siberia rewrite the plague's timeline, dating back to over 5,500 years ago
items.summary: Scientists have found the oldest known evidence of the plague, which sparked deadly outbreaks dating back about 5,500 years ago—some 200 years earlier than previously thought.

items.title: Common nanostructures may explain shared photoproperties in two widespread dark materials
items.summary: A newly developed framework for understanding the photoproperties of both natural organic matter and eumelanin, a natural pigment responsible for dark colors in organisms, may inspire advanced sustainable technologies, scientists say.

items.title: BC and Alberta fall behind on fracking safety distances for residents
items.summary: In May, Prime Minister Mark Carney announced plans to double the capacity of Canada's electricity grid by 2050, using natural gas in the name of "powering Canada strong." Almost all Canadian natural gas these days is derived from hydraulic fracturing—known as fracking—an industrial process invo

items.title: Greenland meltwater adds to AMOC weakening, but updated model finds no tipping point in sight
items.summary: The state of the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) has been a hot topic among climate scientists in recent years. The AMOC is crucial for climate regulation because it pulls warm surface water from the tropics north and sends colder, deeper water south, redistributing large amounts

items.title: VIDEO Veliko otkriće u Izraelu: Pronađena špilja zapečaćena stotinama tisuća godina
items.summary: U SJEVERNOM Izraelu otkrivena je 400.000 godina stara, iznimno očuvana prapovijesna špilja. Nalazište nudi uvid u slabo poznato razdoblje ljudske evolucije prije pojave neandertalaca.

items.title: A rare dinosaur fossil from Antarctica is found tucked away in a drawer
items.summary: Scientists have stumbled on a rare dinosaur fossil from Antarctica, tucked away for decades in a drawer.

items.title: Bacterial protein reveals a hidden rule for controlling calcium
items.summary: A small change in acidity can transform the world around us. A squeeze of lemon changes the taste of food. Vinegar preserves vegetables. Stomach acid helps break down a meal. These familiar effects come from protons—tiny charged particles that can reshape chemical interactions.

items.title: Znanstvenici otkrili novu vrstu prapovijesnog grabežljivca
items.summary: NOVA vrsta prapovijesnog predatora, Paludocyon moyasolai, identificirana je iz lubanje pronađene prije 30 godina kod Barcelone. Riječ je o medvjedopsu iz miocena, teškom 50-70 kg.

items.title: Rising seas make once-rare coastal floods 12 times more likely
items.summary: Extreme floods that once swamped coastal communities only rarely are becoming far more common as climate change caused by humans pushes sea levels higher, according to new research published Wednesday. Experts say the findings are crucial for making plans about floods and coastal infrastructure as t

items.title: Jesu li ovakve vrućine u Europi iznimka ili novo normalno?
items.summary: KOLIKO vrućinama pridonose Sunčev maksimum i super-El Niño?

items.title: Astronomers may have caught an early galaxy in the process of dying
items.summary: Astronomers have spotted many "red and dead" galaxies in the early universe. These are massive systems that stopped forming stars surprisingly early in cosmic history. Now, they may have found evidence of one in the act of becoming dead: a massive galaxy being stripped of its star-forming gas just 1

items.title: Trained AI outperforms biologists at spotting salmon lice
items.summary: Researchers have taken over 120,000 images of salmon lice larvae in seawater and used them to train AI models. The models were much faster and more accurate than experienced biologists at identifying the parasites that feed on the skin and blood of salmonids.

items.title: A young gull's mottled brown plumage acts as a 'not a threat' signal to territorial adults
items.summary: Birds are known for their distinctive plumage that helps them attract mates during the breeding season. For some birds, the path to adulthood is quite linear. One day they are chicks, and a few months later they are fully grown adults with their mature plumage, all set to find and fight for a mate.

items.title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
items.summary: Red, white, and blue stars glitter like a sparkler being waved on a dark night in this new image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.

items.title: Riječki profesor vodi najveće astronomsko snimanje svih vremena
items.summary: NAKON dva desetljeća razvoja i izgradnje, u Opservatoriju Vera C. Rubin u Čileu počelo je najveće astronomsko snimanje svih vremena - desetogodišnje pregledno snimanje neba u projektu Legacy Survey of Space and Time (LSST), a voditelj projekta je riječki profesor Željko Ivezić.

items.title: El Nino set to be 'strong', UN warns
items.summary: El Nino is here and will quickly develop into a strong event between July and September, fueling the likelihood of extreme weather, the United Nations' climate agency warned Friday.

items.title: One for the history books: What we know about the European heat wave
items.summary: Europe is still taking stock of a powerful heat wave in late June, but experts are already confident it ranks among the worst ever recorded—even rivaling a freak 2003 episode.

items.title: Scientists find yeast in ancient Iceman's guts—and make bread
items.summary: Yeast has been growing in the guts of a frozen mummy called Oetzi the Iceman for thousands of years, scientists have discovered, telling AFP they used it to make a tasty sourdough bread.

items.title: New species of ghost shark may have been found in Costa Rica
items.summary: Costa Rican scientists may have discovered a new species of ghost shark in Pacific waters near Cabo Blanco and Cano Island.

items.title: Dangerous heat wave roasts America's big birthday party
items.summary: A punishing heat wave threatened America's July Fourth celebrations, World Cup matches and power grids as near-record temperatures scorched the eastern United States on Friday.

items.title: Osvojila je dva Nobela. Na kraju je vlastito otkriće koštalo života
items.summary: MARIE Curie bila je jedna od najvažnijih znanstvenica 20. stoljeća. Poznata je po otkriću radioaktivnosti.
